evals.prompt: Audit the agent skill in active-skills/gcloud against the specification.
evals.expected_output: The agent locates the gcloud skill, runs scripts/audit_skill.py for the mechanical pre-pass, scores it against the rubric dimensions with cited evidence, runs the triggering test on the description, and produces a ranked review report.
evals.trap: Without the skill, the agent eyeballs SKILL.md and gives vague impressions, skipping objective checks (path integrity, secret scan, script safety) and the triggering test.

assertions.description: Runs scripts/audit_skill.py against the target skill directory.
Evaluates the skill across the rubric dimensions (triggering, structure, content, paths, scripts, security, scoping, freshness) with cited locations.
Produces a ranked report with severity-ordered findings, each carrying a location and a concrete fix.

evals.prompt: Review my SKILL.md in active-skills/refresh-skills and fix whatever isn't following best practices.
evals.expected_output: The agent audits the skill, presents findings, then implements the fixes: mechanical/unambiguous ones directly, judgment calls proposed first, snapshots before editing, and re-runs the audit to verify the fixes landed.
evals.trap: The agent stops at a review and hands back a list of suggestions instead of implementing the improvements and re-verifying them.

assertions.description: Both diagnoses AND implements improvements rather than only reporting.
Snapshots the skill before editing and re-runs audit_skill.py to verify fixes.
Proposes judgment-call edits (e.g. description rewrite, re-scoping) before committing them.

evals.prompt: Before I share this skill with my team, can you check it for security issues and weak triggering?
evals.expected_output: The agent runs the secret/adversarial-instruction scan and network audit, runs the triggering test on the description, and reports blockers (any hardcoded secret or covert instruction escalated regardless of other scores) plus triggering improvements.
evals.trap: Without the skill, the agent gives a generic thumbs-up without scanning for secrets, over-broad tool grants, covert instructions, or testing whether the description actually triggers.

assertions.description: Performs a security pass (secret scan, network/egress audit, least-privilege, adversarial-instruction check).
Runs the 3-trigger / 3-near-miss triggering test on the description.
Escalates any hardcoded secret or covert instruction to Blocker regardless of other scores.

evals.prompt: I want to create a brand-new skill for rotating PDFs from scratch. Where do I start?
evals.expected_output: This is a near-miss. Creating a skill from scratch is skill-creator-enhanced's job, not this skill's. The agent should not run the improvement/audit workflow on a non-existent skill.
evals.trap: The skill over-triggers on the word 'skill' and tries to audit something that doesn't exist yet.

assertions.description: Does NOT invoke the skill-improvement audit workflow (defers to skill creation).
